sessions.intent: Carve the singular-namespaces plan: rename app/** to singular+lowercase AND reshape app.X into collection-node accessors that kill the App* OBP wrappers, make app/context non-null, and promote type.@this to an entity owned by data.Type — all in one branch.

before.assumptions: Started as a folder/namespace rename (brief scope: PLang/app/** only, no logic). Design conversation with Ingi expanded it: the four App* wrapper aliases (AppGoals/AppChannels/AppEvents/AppModules) are OBP violations, so the rename must also reshape app.X into a collection node with [name]/.list/.current selectors; data owns its type; app and context are non-null invariants; type.@this should become a real entity behind data.Type. Ingi chose one branch for all four pieces.
before.risk: Large blast radius. Rename touches ~190 files (mostly alias-shielded). Accessor reshape touches ~286 registry call sites (Types 80, Variables 63, Channels 37 are the heavy ones). Source generator has STRING-LITERAL namespace refs and a tracking-name constant (prefix="app.modules.") the compiler won't catch — a miss there breaks generated-code compilation at consumer sites, not in the generator. Making app/context non-null will surface real un-stamped-Data reads as NREs (intended: they are the bugs the ?. hid). type.@this promotion reshapes the builder schema path.

actions.context: Pulled origin/singular-namespaces, read the brief, merged origin/runtime2 in (clean; touched channels/actor/this.cs only — no plan impact). Verified the brief's rename tables and the App* wrapper claims against the live tree. Found the generator does NOT auto-wire app nav properties (hand-written in app/this.cs), and the App* names are referenced in only 3-4 files each (alias-shielded).

Deep design walk with Ingi across type, channel, goal. Settled: app.X = collection node; .current only where execution has a current (callstack); registry=selection+lifecycle, behavior on element; data owns its type; app+context non-null. Resolved the 'where does the list live' knot: bare app.goal can't be the current without orphaning the collection — current rides as .current. _goals/_byPath are NOT duplicate (PrPath vs Path keys) — kept.

Wrote the spine + four deep-dive topic files. Inventory gathered via four parallel read-only scans (rename map, ~286 accessor call sites by subsystem, ~39 nullability sites + 3 nullable App back-refs + 9 nullable Context fields, generator strings + test mirrors + doc refs). Corrected two brief errors (PascalCase infra folders stay; serializers/channel-events collapse to singular).

Carved four stages in strict dependency order (1 rename → 2 non-null → 3 accessor → 4 type-entity), build green between each. Wrote test handoff: strategy (regression floor + 4 integration cuts) + coverage matrix/failure matrix/new-surfaces. Stage 4 (type-entity, builder schema reshape) flagged as the integration risk and natural cut point.

after.health: green — plan carved, no code changed
after.notes: Plan is design-complete and ready for test-designer then coder. Open item for coder+test-designer: the index-miss policy setting (name + default). Stage 4 is the integration risk (builder LLM schema must be golden-pinned). The generator string literals are the highest-risk mechanical item in stage 1 — verify by clean rebuild. CLAUDE.md OBP-block token updates filed for the docs bot (do not edit CLAUDE.md directly).

sessions.intent: Address Ingi's review comments on the plan, then recreate the stage files and test-handoff files with the review changes baked in.

before.assumptions: Ingi reviewed the five plan files via the review server and left comments; he then deleted the stage/test files for a focused design review and, once comments were addressed, asked to recreate them.
before.risk: Stage/test files must stay consistent with the revised design (module not demoted; ctx→context + extra nullable back-refs; type=PLang System.Type with data.Type.ClrType). Any drift between the deep dives and the stage files would mislead the coder.

actions.context: Addressed 5 review comments (all agreed): module keeps this.cs as a no-.current service at app.module (reversed my demote); ctx→context everywhere + swept for other over-nullable back-refs (5 structural flip, 2 init-only held); type.@this = PLang's System.Type; data.Type.ClrType (CLR type lives on the entity). Replied + resolved each via the review-server API.
Recreated the four stage files and the two test-handoff files with the review changes baked in. Restored stage/test links in plan.md and the stage-status table in summary.md.

after.health: green — plan + stages + tests consistent, no code changed
after.notes: Plan is complete and ready for test-designer. All review comments resolved. Stage files and test handoff reflect the revised design (module not demoted, ctx→context, type entity = PLang System.Type with data.Type.ClrType).

sessions.intent: Translate architect's 4-stage singular-namespaces plan into test contracts: C# TUnit + PLang .test.goal signatures with Assert.Fail / throw 'not implemented' bodies covering accessor reshape, non-null invariant, type entity move, channel I/O relocation, index-miss errors, and rename integration.

before.assumptions: Architect plan complete and consistent (plan.md + 4 stage files + 4 deep dives + test-strategy + test-coverage). plang-types already shipped the type entity at app.data.type with .ClrType, so stage 4 is primarily a move+fold (regression-pinned). Existing C# tests use TUnit Assert.That fluent. PLang test infrastructure under Tests/ with .test.goal naming.
before.risk: Coverage gaps if I only mirror the architect's matrix without thinking independently — per memory rule, I should go slow with new areas. The accessor surface has many small selectors; risk of low-value tests that just exercise indexers. Real risk lies in: index-miss negative tests across all collections, channel I/O after registry switch removal, non-null surfacing un-stamped data bugs, builder schema golden parity.

actions.context: Read the architect's full plan: spine + 4 deep dives + 4 stage files + 2 test-handoff files. Surveyed existing test directories (PLang.Tests/App/** and Tests/**) — saw TUnit `[Test]` + `Assert.That(...)` fluent + `PLangEngine = global::app.@this` alias as the established style. Confirmed coverage matrix has 17 accessor rows + 6 nullability + 7 type-entity + 4 rename + 6 failure modes + 4 integration cuts.

Wrote the test-designer plan. 8 batches (~52 C# + 5 PLang + 1 schema golden). Layer mapping: C# pins internal @this surface, PLang pins developer-facing observable behavior, integration cut for builder schema. Flagged 4 open items for Ingi: index-miss exception type, app.module.current absence guard, golden baseline location, goal-registry-no-IO sibling test.

52 C# TUnit tests across 9 files. All bodies are Assert.Fail('Not implemented'); names + comments are the spec. Covers: app.goal/channel/type/module/event/format/variable/error/navigator accessors; index-miss negatives across goal/channel/type/module; non-null app/context invariant + un-stamped data.Type throws + static fallback removed + structural back-refs + app.Parent stays nullable; type entity at type.@this with folded Entry fields + data/Converter.cs deleted; builder schema golden (integration cut 3); rename build+run end-to-end (integration cut 1). Open items resolved with judgment: index-miss exception left as Throws<Exception> for coder to pin; module.current absence as reflection probe; golden baseline inline.

5 PLang .test.goal files for developer-facing observable surface. One goal per file (memory rule: builder overwrites multi-goal files). Bodies = `- throw 'not implemented'`; second-line comment is the spec. Covers cuts 1+2+4 surface from PLang author perspective.
Test-plan documents files written + coverage mapping + open-item resolutions + coder notes. Verdict pass:true. Summary written per character convention.

after.health: green — test contract complete, ready for coder
after.notes: 52 C# tests + 5 PLang test goals + 1 builder schema golden contract. All bodies are Assert.Fail / throw 'not implemented'. Coder owns: pinning index-miss exception type, the builder schema baseline (inline or extend existing Tests/Types/ golden), test implementations stage-by-stage following architect's strict dependency order (1→2→3→4).

sessions.intent: Five-pass review of the singular-namespace rename branch at HEAD.

before.assumptions: Coder v1 report listed Stage-4 fold-dissolve and property sweep as deferred; expected a partial, inconsistent state.
before.risk: 825 files changed; risk concentrated in new accessor registries and the type-entity Entry fold, not the mechanical renames.

actions.context: Reconciled coder report vs HEAD — report is STALE; 4 commits land after it. Entry/EntryKind structs gone, plural App.* props gone, Variable->@this done. Analyzed actual HEAD f7790b3a6.
Clean rebuild: 0 errors, 510 pre-existing nullability warnings (deferred Stage-2). No new warning classes.

Surveyed all 16 list-registries. CLEAN: no type-switching in any registry (architect's main risk resolved); index-miss hard-throw (KeyNotFoundException) consistent across selection registries goal/channel/format/type/module; variable/navigator soft-return is correct PLang semantics.

FINDING (NEEDS WORK): app.Type[name]/of<T>() return new @this(typeName) with no Context (type/list/this.cs:161,172). All catalog props route through Context -> silently null off that door; ClrType falls to static map (null for DLL types). data.Type door DOES stamp (data/this.cs:81) -> doors diverge despite type/this.cs:13 doc claiming equivalence. TypeAccessorTests passes only by manual t.Context=app.User.Context on every fold case -> test asserts the workaround. Root: catalog data is App-global but reached via actor Context; registry has no Context anchor.
LOW: Promote() re-walks entire action catalog + linear self-scan per stamped entity not built by BuildTypeEntries; BuildTypeEntries not memoized. Re-derive smell in time.
LOW (deletion test): redundant enumerators beside new canonical `list` — goal/list.Value (:299, unused) and channel/list.All (:154, unused, born redundant in Stage-3 commit 273f51ad4).

after.health: Build clean, rename well-executed (Entry dissolved, no registry type-switching, index-miss consistent). One undisclosed behavioral gap blocks: type-entity door asymmetry with silent-null catalog props + test asserting the workaround. Two dead enumerators.
after.notes: Verdict FAIL -> next bot coder. See v1/report.md + v1/verdict.json.

sessions.intent: Re-review the coder's fix for v1 findings + fresh-eye pass, grounded by running tests.

before.assumptions: Coder fixed v1 door asymmetry + dead enumerators; expected door fix to be sound.
before.risk: The fix introduced a memoized cache; caches over reflection-ordered sets are a determinism risk.

actions.context: FINDING (FAIL): door fix is non-deterministic. _catalogByName built by first-wins dict.TryAdd(entry.Value,entry) (this.cs:175) over BuildTypeEntries(null)->KnownTypes()->.Distinct() and SafeGetTypes()->assembly.GetTypes() (no stable order). Name collision 'goal' -> barren entry can shadow Fields-bearing one. data.Type inherits via Promote->ComplexSchemas (type/this.cs:137).

EMPIRICAL: clean rebuild PlangConsole+PLang.Tests (0 errors). Full suite 3693/3694. AppType_IndexByName_Fields_OnRecordType_FoldedFromEntry fails 8/8 in isolation; passes sometimes co-executed with siblings (process-order dependent). BuilderValidate_CallsBuildOnEachAction_InOrder is a pre-existing parallel-ordering flake (passes isolated).

v1 finding #3: channel/list.All deleted OK, goal/list.Value deleted OK. RESIDUAL (low): goal/list.All kept with zero callers behind a misleading comment ('non-IEnumerable alias' but it IS IEnumerable). Delete or fix comment.
CLEAN: emit {TypeName}? on public partial when IsNullable while backing field stays non-? for init. Correctly matches hand-written partial declaration. Minimal, well-scoped.

after.health: Reshape sound; door fix for v1#1 traded a silent null for a non-deterministic null. Proof-test fails 8/8 isolated on clean build. One dead enumerator (goal/list.All) remains. Generator + perf fixes clean.
after.notes: Verdict FAIL -> next bot coder. Root: first-wins cache over unordered type set with name collisions. See v2/report.md + v2/verdict.json.

sessions.intent: Re-review the coder's fix for the v2 non-determinism blocker; verify in isolation.

before.assumptions: Coder added deterministic collision resolution + corrected goal/list.All comment.
before.risk: Determinism fixes must be verified the way they failed — in isolation, not just full-suite.

actions.context: v2 blocker FIXED: first-wins TryAdd replaced by richness-rank tiebreak (Record>Enum>Scalar>barren, this.cs:172-205). Richer entry wins regardless of reflection order. Rank() reading fold getters during cache build with Context==null is safe (Promote short-circuits, no re-entry).

VERIFIED the v2 failure mode: clean rebuild; AppType_IndexByName_Fields_OnRecordType_FoldedFromEntry isolation x8 -> 8 PASS/0 FAIL (was 0/8). Types namespace 290/290. Full suite 3694/3694, 0 failed. PlangConsole + PLang.Tests build clean.
v2 finding #2 was MY false positive: goal/list.All has a caller (GoalsTests.cs:233 goals.All); my v2 grep missed the local-var call site. Coder corrected the comment accurately. Owned, dropped.

after.health: Build clean, suite 3694/3694, formerly non-deterministic fold-prop contract now deterministic (8/8 isolated). Singular-namespace reshape clean. One latent non-blocking edge (equal-rank same-name collision).
after.notes: Verdict PASS -> next bot tester. See v3/report.md + v3/verdict.json.

sessions.intent: Validate test honesty for the singular-namespaces refactor; hunt false greens in the inverted Stage 2 nullability tests, the Stage 4 builder golden, and the 5 PLang contract goals.

before.assumptions: Coder report claims 11 failing/Stage4 deferred; codeanalyzer v3 claims 3694/3694 green. report.md is stale (Stage4 Entry-dissolve actually landed in a94d03a54). Need ground-truth run.
before.risk: Coder rewrote test-designer's Stage 2 contract tests to assert the opposite ('Per Ingi') and declared branch complete — highest false-green risk per character.

actions.context: Ran full C# suite (3694: 1 flaky fail, see F6) + PLang suite (253/253). report.md stale vs HEAD; Stage 4 actually landed. Read all 52 C# + 5 PLang contract tests against intent.

F1 CRITICAL: all 7 Stage 2 tests inverted vs architect spec (still 'pending', fallback-removal design intact), citing unrecorded 'Per Ingi' reversal. Traced through architect record — no endorsement found.
F2 CRITICAL: byte-identical golden asserts only tautological non-null checks; TypeSchemas/ToJson never null. No diff. The architect's 'gate' can never fail.

F4/F5: DataTypeReadsEntity never reads .Type; ChannelIndexMissThrows passes unset var as channel (null, not index-miss) and only asserts sawError==true, never Error.Key. Read .pr to confirm built module/action.
Wrote findings, verdict (fail), coverage extract, summary.

after.notes: 8 findings (2 critical, 4 major, 2 minor). Stage 1 + Stage 4 structural move genuine; behavioral contracts inverted/tautological. F1 needs Ingi ruling on whether Stage 2 is cut.

sessions.intent: Review coder v2's fundamental changes (non-null Context/Type, type.Null sentinel, Promote throw, producer stamping) responding to tester v1.

before.assumptions: Coder v2 flipped Data.Context + Data.Type non-null via null!/sentinel, made Promote() throw on unstamped fold reads, and rewrote tester's false-green tests. Need to confirm the non-null invariant holds at all production read sites and the test rewrites are honest, not re-greened.
before.risk: Non-null-via-null! lies to the compiler; a missed producer NREs. Promote throw could fire during cache build (codeanalyzer v3 relied on it short-circuiting). String-magic Null sentinel could collide with user type names.

actions.context: Verified the Promote() throw is safe during cache build: every BuildTypeEntries entry uses the 2-arg ctor (_foldLoaded=true) so Rank()->Fields->Promote() early-returns before the Context check. My v3 PASS safety claim still holds via _foldLoaded. F1: IsNull uses string-magic Value=='null' instead of ReferenceEquals(this,Null) — latent literal-name footgun, recommend one-line fix. F4: Scheme getter lost null-safety (bare NRE).

Data.Context flipped non-null via null! (field genuinely holds null in unstamped test fixtures — acceptable set-after-construction pattern). Null-sentinel Type getter/setter consistent. F3: As(string typeName) dropped its GetPrimitiveOrMime fallback, contradicting ValidateBuild/Sqlite which kept it; no production caller so latent.

Producer stamping is root-cause at the producer (Permission stamps Context on rehydrated grants; Sqlite/set route through entity's own ClrType resolver) — not consumer patching. Correct OBP direction.

Verified tester's false-greens are fixed not re-greened. F2 golden is real SHA256 compare with length guards. F1 nullability tests honestly flipped to architect spec direction. Ran subsets myself: golden 2/2, nullability 7/7, DataTests 310/310, build 0 errors. F2-finding: one test name (ThrowsHard) overpromises vs its IsNull assertion.

after.notes: Fundamentals (non-null Context/Type, type.Null sentinel, Promote throw, producer stamping) sound; cache-build safe via _foldLoaded; build clean + affected subsets green (verified, not claimed). 4 minor/latent notes; F1 ReferenceEquals for sentinel worth a one-liner. Next: tester.

sessions.intent: Validate coder v2 fixes to tester v1's 7 findings are honest green, not false green.

before.assumptions: Coder claims F1-F7 fixed, C# 3694/3694, PLang 253/253. codeanalyzer v4 PASS.
before.risk: Review-driven fixes are highest false-green risk; the new Promote() throw is the headline change most likely to be untested.

actions.context: Clean rebuild then C# 3694/3694 x4 (F6 flake gone) + PLang 253/253. Read every changed test and .pr; builder false-green sweep clean.
Deletion test: replaced Promote() Context==null throw with `return this` -> 3694/3694 still pass. Headline F1 fail-loud contract is uncovered; reverted, git clean.
FAIL verdict: 1 MAJOR false-green (uncovered Promote throw + misnamed test) + 3 minor. 6 of 7 v1 findings honestly fixed.

after.notes: Suite green (C# 3694/3694 x4, PLang 253/253). Single surgical fix: cover the Promote() throw and rename DataType_OnUnstampedData_ThrowsHard_NoSilentFallback.

sessions.intent: Verify coder v3 fixes to tester v2's 1 MAJOR + 3 minor findings are honest (mutation-confirmed), not just green.

before.assumptions: Coder v3 added Promote-throw coverage + primitive carve-out test + file-exists guard + Capture %!data% echo. Claims C# 3696, PLang 253.
before.risk: The new throw tests are themselves the highest false-green risk — must mutation-confirm they catch the deletion, not pass vacuously.

actions.context: Clean rebuild. C# 3696/3696, PLang 253/253 (8 HTTP fails first run = httpbin.org transients, v3 zero production source, cleared on re-run). .pr sweep clean.

Mutation A: remove Promote throw -> only TypeFoldRead_OnUnstampedDomainEntity_ThrowsHard fails. Mutation B: delete _foldLoaded=true -> only TypeFoldRead_OnPrimitiveEntity_DoesNotThrow fails. Both new throw tests deletion-confirmed honest. Reverted, git clean.
PASS verdict. All 4 v2 findings resolved and mutation-confirmed. type/this.cs coverage 62.3->78.3% line.

after.notes: All v2 findings closed with deletion-confirmed tests. C# 3696/3696, PLang 253/253. Ready for security review.

sessions.intent: Cross-cutting audit of singular-namespaces refactor — verify codeanalyzer v4 + tester v3 verdicts; find gaps in the seams.

before.assumptions: Codeanalyzer v4 PASS on coder v2 fundamentals; tester v3 PASS on coder v3 (tests + capture goal only). No security review.
before.risk: v3 changed tests only — codeanalyzer v4's 4 latent findings (F1 IsNull, F3 As(string) fallback drop, F4 Scheme NRE) remain in production code, unaddressed.

actions.context: Auditor v1 outputs — cross-cutting review of singular-namespaces. PASS with 5 minor/nit findings (3 already flagged by codeanalyzer v4 as latent, 1 security routing gap, 1 cross-file invariant note for docs).

Spot-checked codeanalyzer v4's latent findings F1/F3/F4 in HEAD (all still present, none promoted to real bugs); traced producer stamping via Data.Context setter propagating onto _type.Context; verified type/this.cs entity + type/list/this.cs registry split matches architect's stage-4 plan; confirmed _foldLoaded=true in 2-arg ctor keeps cache build under Promote's short-circuit.

Clean rebuilt PlangConsole (0 errors, 254 pre-existing warnings). Ran TUnit suite: 3696/3696 pass in 41s. Ran plang --test from Tests/: 245 pass + 8 HTTP transients (httpbin.org flake, same shape tester v3 reported). Test green is real.

after.health: green — fundamentals consistent across seams, 3696/3696 C# pass, PLang HTTP flakes are transient external network issues
after.notes: 5 findings, all minor/nit. F1: route through security before docs. F2-F4: codeanalyzer-v4 latent items (one-line cleanups available, not blocking). F5: producer-stamping invariant worth documenting in good_to_know.md.

sessions.intent: First security pass on singular-namespaces (47 commits, 860 files). Validate Stage-1 rename did not break security invariants; review Stage-2 nullability flip (data._context = null!), Stage-3 throw-on-miss accessors, Stage-4 type entity reshape + DLL loader + Promote throw paths.

before.assumptions: Bot trail ends at tester v3 PASS / codeanalyzer v4 PASS (green and honest). Branch is mostly mechanical rename; the four staged semantic changes are the only places real regressions could land. No prior security review on this branch.
before.risk: Stage-2 nullability flip removed _context==null guards in data/this.Transport.cs and elsewhere. Stage-3 throw-on-miss could become DoS if PLang user input reaches the throwing indexers. Stage-4 Promote throws InvalidOperationException on unstamped non-primitive reads. Rename could have broken signing/wire/AuthGate paths.

actions.context: semgrep clean against baseline (15 known INFO + 1 new INFO at builder/type/Render.cs — build-time JSON renderer with no sensitive graph reachable, accepted).

Stage-4 type entity + DLL loader gate + Stage-1 path rename. Verified Promote throws are developer-loud (not user-input DoS); AuthGate canonicalization gate (closed in purge-systemio-from-actions/v2) intact via FilePath ctor Canonicalize → PathHelper.GetFullPath; LoadAssemblyAsync limited to documented accepted-risk callers (module/add, code/load, code/this.Snapshot).

9-step VerifyAsync pipeline intact after rename: type → freshness → expiry → nonce replay → contracts → headers (FixedTimeEquals constant-time) → data-hash → signature. ToSigningBytes thread-safety unchanged. Signature class shape preserved.

Stage-2 nullability flip walked: data._context = null! with removed _context==null guards in Wrap/Compress. Today self-protecting via Type.Kind null-coalesce — F1 low residual. Wire depth limits intact (MaxReadDepth 64, MaxNormalizeDepth 128). Wire.Read does not stamp Context — F3 low residual. Channel handler null-context force — F2 low residual.

Standing findings re-checked after rename. Variables.Snapshot no-[Sensitive], callback.run signing-skip gap, AppChannels.Channel(string) parity gap, MigrationEnvelope keyless Signature, Channel.Stream.ReadAllBytesAsync no Buffer — all unchanged by rename, same severity, same residual posture.
Wrote v1 summary: PASS verdict. Zero high, zero medium open. Three low residuals (F1/F2/F3) — all latent on future expansion, not reachable today.

after.notes: Zero high/medium. Three low residuals are documentation gaps, not code defects. Stage-1 rename preserved every security invariant; Stage-2/3/4 semantic changes are safely contained in current call graphs. Next bot: docs.

sessions.intent: Auditor v2 — capture security v1 (pushed after my v1), retract v1 F1 stale-state finding, establish pipeline-state rule.

before.assumptions: v1 ran without git fetch; security had completed locally but hadn't pushed.
before.risk: v1's F1 (no security review) was stale-state, not real. Need to retract and incorporate security's findings.

actions.context: Auditor v2: retract v1 F1, cross-reference security's 3 lows (F1 + F3 echo my F5 producer-stamping invariant). Net 4 findings, PASS, next is docs.
Saved feedback memory: before auditing, fetch + verify all upstream bots (coder/codeanalyzer/tester/security) ran on current version. If missing, status:blocked, stop.
Proposed character change: add Process step 0 (fetch + check all upstream bots), document blocked verdict shape.

after.health: green — security v1 PASS reinforces my v1 findings; pipeline-state rule captured to prevent recurrence
after.notes: Net 4 minor/nit findings (down from 5). Next bot: docs.
